varieties.name: Valencia
varieties.origin: Developed by William Wolfskill in Santa Ana, California, mid-1800s from Portuguese stock
varieties.traits: Medium-large fruit, 2.7-3 inches diameter, smooth thin rind, bright orange when ripe
varieties.flesh_fruit: Deep orange, very juicy, sweet with slight tartness, excellent flavor. Few to many seeds
varieties.yield_notes: High productivity, holds well on tree for extended period
varieties.growing_notes: Ideal for Brisbane subtropical climate. Main harvest December-June. Known as best juicing orange. Thrives in humid subtropical conditions. Crops exceptionally well in Queensland. Can carry two crops simultaneously
varieties.availability: Widely available from Queensland nurseries, Daley's Fruit Nursery, local garden centers

varieties.name: Seedless Valencia
varieties.origin: Bud sport mutation of Valencia discovered in California
varieties.traits: Similar to Valencia, slightly earlier maturity, virtually seedless
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sweet, very juicy, excellent for fresh juice. No seeds to minimal seeds

varieties.name: Washington Navel
varieties.origin: Brazil 1820s, brought to California 1873. One of oldest commercial navels, parent tree still producing in Riverside, California
varieties.traits: Large, round fruit with characteristic navel. Bright orange, thick pebbled rind, easy to peel
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sweet, low acid, excellent eating quality. Seedless. Easy to segment
varieties.yield_notes: Good producer but fruit should be used when ripe as prone to splitting in humid climates

varieties.name: Lane's Late Navel
varieties.origin: Australian selection, bud sport discovered in NSW
varieties.traits: Similar to Washington Navel, thin smooth rind. Fruit holds longer on tree
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sweet, seedless, excellent eating quality
varieties.growing_notes: Matures 2 months later than Washington (July-October). Great companion to Washington Navel for extended harvest. Popular in Southeast Queensland. Can store on tree up to 3 weeks longer than Washington
varieties.availability: Queensland nurseries, Daley's Fruit, Burke's Backyard recommended varieties

varieties.name: Navelina
varieties.origin: Spanish variety, early-maturing selection
varieties.traits: Large oblong fruit, excellent quality. Outstanding deep orange-red skin color, very attractive
varieties.flesh_fruit: Excellent quality, brilliant color, very early in season
varieties.yield_notes: Regular and good cropper in subtropical areas. Similar yields to Washington Navel
varieties.growing_notes: One of earliest navels to fruit. Naturally develops outstanding color. Performs excellently in Brisbane subtropical conditions. Good fruit size
varieties.availability: Daley's Fruit Nursery, specialized Queensland citrus suppliers

varieties.name: Navelate
varieties.origin: Spanish late-season navel variety
varieties.traits: Slightly oblong fruit, excellent flavor and rind texture. Can be slightly thorny when young
varieties.flesh_fruit: Excellent quality, consistent flavor
varieties.growing_notes: Matures same time as Washington but can store mature on tree about 3 weeks longer. Extends navel season in Brisbane gardens
varieties.availability: Daley's Fruit, select Queensland nurseries

varieties.name: Arnold Blood Orange
varieties.origin: Developed by Mike Arnold in South Australia, 1955. Sport of Moro blood orange
varieties.traits: Medium size, red-purple colored flesh and rind blush. Pebbled texture, somewhat difficult to peel
varieties.flesh_fruit: Deep red to maroon, sweet-tangy-tart with berry notes reminiscent of strawberries and raspberries. Sweet floral aroma. 9-11 segments

varieties.name: Cara Cara
varieties.origin: Discovered 1976 at Hacienda Cara Cara in Valencia, Venezuela
varieties.traits: Navel variety, smaller than Washington Navel but similar appearance externally
varieties.flesh_fruit: Pink to red flesh similar to Star Ruby grapefruit. Very sweet, low acid. Seedless
varieties.yield_notes: Good producer, fruit often grows quite large
varieties.growing_notes: Does NOT need cold winter to develop color - ideal for Brisbane and warmer Queensland areas. Matures slightly earlier than Washington Navel. Becoming very popular in Australia. Excellent eating orange with unique color
varieties.availability: Daley's Fruit, Sunshine Citrus, specialized Queensland nurseries

varieties.name: Joppa
varieties.origin: Traditional variety, heavy mid-season bearer
varieties.traits: Medium fruit, traditional orange appearance
varieties.flesh_fruit: Very juicy, commercial juicing quality. Has seeds
varieties.growing_notes: Bears fruit AFTER Valencia crops finish, extending season. Recommended for Southeast Queensland with Valencia for 6 months of fruit. Commercial juicing variety adapted to home gardens
varieties.availability: Sunshine Citrus, select Queensland citrus specialists

varieties.name: Hamlin
varieties.origin: Chance seedling near Glenwood, Florida 1879, named for owner A.G. Hamlin
varieties.traits: Small to medium, smooth thin poorly-colored peel. Round shape
varieties.flesh_fruit: Pale yellow juice, mild flavor, juicy. Nearly seedless (0-6 seeds)

varieties.name: Parson Brown
varieties.origin: Florida heirloom, late 1800s. Most popular orange in Florida before modern varieties
varieties.traits: Slightly rough skin, early-season ripening
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sweet-tart flavor, high juice content, perfect for fresh juice
varieties.growing_notes: Florida heirloom variety with over 140 years history. Adapted to humid conditions. Some small growers still cultivate to preserve legacy. Early season variety for subtropical climates
varieties.availability: Rare in Australia, may need to source from specialty heirloom citrus collectors

varieties.name: Pineapple Orange
varieties.origin: Citra, Florida from seed 1860. Named for aromatic pineapple-like fragrance
varieties.traits: Medium size, 2.75-3 inches, slightly flattened ends. Smooth peel, can develop red-orange color in cool nights
varieties.flesh_fruit: Incredibly sweet and tropical flavor. Excellent juice color and quality. Seedy (15-25 seeds)
varieties.growing_notes: Florida heirloom 160+ years old. Intense sweetness and fragrant aroma. Thin skin made it unsuitable for long-distance shipping, leading to decline. Rare heritage variety worth preserving. Mid-season harvest
varieties.availability: Rare, specialty Florida heirloom collectors, limited availability Australia

varieties.name: Tarocco
varieties.origin: Italian blood orange variety from Sicily
varieties.traits: Very large fruit, excellent appearance
varieties.flesh_fruit: Great flavor, blood orange characteristics
varieties.yield_notes: Slow to come into production but productive once established
varieties.growing_notes: Can be grown in Brisbane but Arnold Blood is more reliable for subtropical conditions. Requires Mediterranean-like climate ideally but some success in Queensland with proper care
varieties.availability: Victorian Citrus Farms, Yalca Fruit Trees, select Australian nurseries

varieties.name: Moro
varieties.origin: Italian variety, most pigmented blood orange
varieties.traits: Medium to large, deep red interior and exterior blush
varieties.flesh_fruit: Most intensely colored blood orange, sweet-tart flavor

varieties.name: Bergamot (Sour Orange)
varieties.origin: Southern Italy, heritage variety. Also known as Castagnaro
varieties.traits: Most fragrant of all citrus. Small tree, attractive
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sour flesh, not for eating fresh. Used in marmalades and baking
varieties.yield_notes: Good ornamental and productive for rind use
varieties.growing_notes: Rind oil used in Earl Grey Tea, Turkish Delight, health products, cosmetics. More frost hardy than other citrus. Wonderful small ornamental tree. Traditional heritage variety with cultural significance
varieties.availability: Daley's Fruit Nursery, specialty Queensland citrus suppliers

varieties.name: Seville (Bitter Orange)
varieties.origin: Southeast Asia, spread to Europe via Moorish Spain. Named after Seville, Spain
varieties.traits: Medium fruit, bitter flavor, traditional marmalade orange
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sour and bitter, pairs well with sugar sweetness
varieties.yield_notes: Hardy, vigorous, more frost hardy than other citrus

varieties.name: Nagpur Orange (Santra) 🏆
varieties.origin: Nagpur, Maharashtra, India. Indigenous to India, also called Nagpur Mandarin or Ponkan type
varieties.traits: Large loose-jacket mandarin, 134-150g, 60-70mm diameter. Medium-thick rind, orange-colored, prominent sunken oil glands
varieties.flesh_fruit: Orange flesh, tender, melting, very juicy (45% juice), mild and pleasant aromatic flavor. 8-10 segments, 18-20 seeds. Sweet with 0.6-0.7% citric acid
varieties.yield_notes: Productive but alternate bearing tendency. Peak production 10-20 years
varieties.growing_notes: ONE OF THE BEST MANDARINS IN THE WORLD. Geographic Indication status (GI tag) 2014. Perfect for tropical monsoon. Blossoms during monsoon, matures February-March (Mrig crop) for export. India's most famous orange. Climatically one of most tropical mandarins. Performs excellently in Kerala conditions
varieties.availability: Greens of Kerala, widespread availability in Kerala nurseries, Maharashtra suppliers

varieties.name: Coorg Orange (Kodagu Orange) 🏆
varieties.origin: Coorg (Kodagu), Karnataka, India. Believed introduced from Central India 300 years ago. GI status 2006
varieties.traits: Greenish-yellow color, medium size (smaller than Nagpur), tight skin, well-blended sugar-acid content. Mandarin hybrid (Citrus reticulata)
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sweet-sour taste, deliciously juicy, rich orange pulp, distinctive sweet-tangy flavor

varieties.name: Khasi Mandarin (Soh Niamtra) 🏆
varieties.origin: Meghalaya, Northeast India (Khasi, Jaintia hills). Indigenous to Indo-Burma biodiversity hotspot. GI tag
varieties.traits: Slightly bigger than tennis ball, 134-150g, 60-70mm diameter. Smooth TIGHT skin (harder to peel than other mandarins), orange-yellow to bright orange, 3-3.5mm thick peel
varieties.flesh_fruit: More flesh and juice than other mandarins. Sweet, aromatic, distinctive quality. Vibrant golden color. 45% juiciness, 0.6-0.7% citric acid - one of sweetest oranges. 8-10 segments
varieties.yield_notes: Makes up 43.6% of India's total citrus production. 38.2% of citrus orchard area
varieties.growing_notes: INDIA'S PRIMARY CITRUS. Indigenous to Northeast. Called 'King of Oranges'. Primary gene center for world's Citrus species. Harvest November-late February/April. Traditional bamboo harvesting tool (proh). Perfect for monsoon regions. Can grow in Kerala with similar conditions. Cultural significance to Khasi and Jaintia people
varieties.availability: Northeast India nurseries, may be available Kerala through specialized suppliers

varieties.name: Sikkim Mandarin 🏆
varieties.origin: Sikkim, India. Native to Himalayan foothills. Closely related to Khasi and Coorg mandarins
varieties.traits: Similar to other Indian mandarins, adapted to mountain/hill conditions
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sweet, juicy, known for excellent quality and taste
varieties.yield_notes: Good production in hill areas 600-1500m elevation
varieties.growing_notes: Indigenous to India. Nucellar clonal budline of Nagpur. Popular in Kolkata markets. Grows in Tista and Rangeet valleys. Could adapt to Kerala hill regions like Munnar, Wayanad
varieties.availability: Sikkim state nurseries, Northeast suppliers

varieties.name: Assam Mandarin 🏆
varieties.origin: Assam, Northeast India. Indigenous variety
varieties.traits: Seedling variety, nucellar clonal budline of Nagpur
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sweet, juicy, typical mandarin characteristics
varieties.yield_notes: Good production in Brahmaputra valley and Dibrugarh districts
varieties.growing_notes: Indigenous to India's Northeast. Adapted to monsoon climate. Could perform in Kerala tropical monsoon conditions. Part of India's traditional orange heritage
varieties.availability: Assam state nurseries, Northeast India suppliers

varieties.name: Kinnow
varieties.origin: Hybrid of King mandarin and Willow Leaf mandarin, developed in California but widely adopted in India
varieties.traits: Glossy skin, attractive appearance, medium size
varieties.flesh_fruit: Tangy-sweet taste, very juicy, excellent for juice
varieties.growing_notes: Very popular in Punjab and Northern India. Grown successfully in Northeast states including areas near Kerala. Adapts to warm climates. Harvest mid-January to mid-February. TSS:Acid ratio 12:1 at maturity
varieties.availability: Widely available Indian nurseries, Kerala suppliers

varieties.name: Mosambi (Sweet Lime, Bathayi) 🌱
varieties.origin: Native to Southern Iran, Southeast Asia. Widely cultivated throughout India. Called 'bathayi' in Kerala Malayalam
varieties.traits: Small tree up to 8m. Oval fruit, green ripening to yellow-green, greenish pulp. Smooth to slightly rough skin. Not a true orange but Citrus limetta
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sweet and mild flavor (essence of lime without tartness). 50mg Vitamin C per 100g. Juice turns bitter quickly in air
varieties.growing_notes: PERFECT FOR KERALA TROPICAL CLIMATE. Most common citrus juice in Indian subcontinent. Refreshing in hot weather. Thrives in tropical and subtropical climates. Popular at mobile road stalls. Rich in antioxidants. Traditional Indian fruit. Maharashtra, Andhra Pradesh, Punjab top producers
varieties.availability: Greens of Kerala, widespread Kerala availability, all India nurseries

varieties.name: Hamlin
varieties.origin: Florida 1879 - same as Brisbane listing
varieties.traits: Small to medium, smooth thin peel. 2.75-3 inch diameter
varieties.flesh_fruit: Pale yellow juice, mild, juicy, 0-6 seeds
varieties.growing_notes: EXCELLENT for Kerala's tropical monsoon climate. Specifically noted as producing satisfactory size fruit in SEMITROPICAL CLIMATES with HIGH HEAT AND HUMIDITY - perfect description of Kerala! Early season October-December. Orange trees produce earlier in humid tropics than subtropics
varieties.availability: May need to source internationally, Florida varieties increasingly available India

varieties.name: Valencia
varieties.origin: California mid-1800s (same as Brisbane listing)
varieties.traits: Medium-large, smooth thin rind, bright orange
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sweet, juicy, excellent for juice. Few to many seeds
varieties.growing_notes: Popular in India. Grown in North and South India including Tamil Nadu. Available in Kerala markets. Summer orange (November-February season in India). Thrives in warm climates zones 9-11. Can handle tropical conditions if humidity managed properly
varieties.availability: Indian nurseries, may be available Kerala through citrus specialists

varieties.name: Darjeeling Orange 🏆
varieties.origin: Darjeeling, West Bengal hill regions. Indigenous to India
varieties.traits: Specialty variety, thin peel
varieties.flesh_fruit: Unique sweetness and fragrance, high juice content

varieties.name: Mudkhed Orange 🌱
varieties.origin: Mudkhed area, Maharashtra, India
varieties.traits: Medium size, bright orange color
varieties.flesh_fruit: Tender, juicy, sweet flavor
varieties.growing_notes: Popular in Maharashtra alongside Nagpur oranges. Regional specialty. Can be used for juice or fresh eating. Indigenous to Maharashtra region
varieties.availability: Maharashtra nurseries, may be sourced to Kerala

varieties.name: Sathgudi Orange 🌱
varieties.origin: Andhra Pradesh and Tamil Nadu, South India
varieties.traits: Smooth skin, attractive appearance
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sweet taste, high juice content
varieties.yield_notes: Popular commercial variety in South India

varieties.name: Tangor (Orange-Mandarin Hybrids)
varieties.origin: Various hybrids between sweet oranges and mandarins
varieties.traits: Characteristics intermediate between oranges and mandarins
varieties.flesh_fruit: Usually sweet, sometimes easier to peel than oranges
varieties.growing_notes: Various tangor types grown in India. Ellendale is example of tangor. Adapted to tropical and subtropical conditions. Available through Indian citrus programs
varieties.availability: Various Indian nurseries

varieties.name: Blood Orange (Various)
varieties.origin: Various blood orange varieties, originally Mediterranean
varieties.traits: Distinctive deep red flesh due to anthocyanin pigments
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sweet with berry-like flavor, red to burgundy interior
varieties.yield_notes: Variable, generally needs cool nights for color
